operators.label: attest
operators.description: Records a verified claim about a referent. Adds attestation metadata or evidence without defining persistence behavior.

operators.label: authorize
operators.description: Grants permission, authority, or normative standing to a subject or referent within a defined scope.

operators.label: bind
operators.description: Associates a referent with a context, bearer, scope, or applicability setting.

operators.label: branch
operators.description: Creates a divergent continuation from a referent, producing one or more forward paths.

operators.label: collapse
operators.description: Reduces structural scale or complexity by collapsing nested, redundant, or expanded structure.

operators.label: copy
operators.description: Produces a duplicate, replica, or copied representation of a referent.

operators.label: embed
operators.description: Places a referent inside a containing structure, establishing containment or embedded placement.

operators.label: expand
operators.description: Increases structural scale, detail, or complexity by expanding a referent into a more elaborated form.

operators.label: link
operators.description: Establishes an association or relation between referents without by itself implying containment or persistence.

operators.label: merge
operators.description: Combines two or more referents or structural components into a unified referent or aggregate.

operators.label: project
operators.description: Derives a partial view, projection, or selected representation of a referent.

operators.label: reorder
operators.description: Changes the ordering or arrangement of components without necessarily changing their membership.

operators.label: revert
operators.description: Returns, restores, or relates a referent to a prior version along a provenance or version chain.

operators.label: shift
operators.description: Relocates a referent across a relation, bearer, context, or structural position while maintaining linkage.

operators.label: split
operators.description: Divides a referent into two or more components, parts, branches, or sub-referents.

operators.label: unbind
operators.description: Removes, releases, or severs a contextual binding between a referent and its current context, bearer, or scope.

operators.label: version
operators.description: Produces or identifies a temporally ordered version or successor of a referent.

families.label: Aggregation
families.description: Transformations that combine multiple referents or structural components into a unified whole.

families.label: Association
families.description: Transformations that establish, alter, or represent relations among referents without implying containment.

families.label: Attestation
families.description: Transformations that record evidence, verification, or claims about a referent.

families.label: Branching
families.description: Transformations that produce divergent continuations from a referent.

families.label: Containment
families.description: Transformations that place a referent within a containing structure or organizational enclosure.

families.label: Contextual
families.description: Transformations that bind, unbind, or otherwise alter contextual placement or applicability.
families.note: No separate Domain/Family/Contextual.lean file was shown; constructor and mappings are confirmed through TransformationFamily and Operator.Semantics.

families.label: Decomposition
families.description: Transformations that divide a referent into constituent components, parts, branches, or sub-referents.

families.label: Migration
families.description: Transformations that relocate a referent across a relation, bearer, context, or structural position while maintaining linkage.

families.label: Normative
families.description: Transformations that apply, alter, or record normative authority, permission, or standing.

families.label: Projection
families.description: Transformations that derive a partial view, selected representation, or projection of a referent.

families.label: Replication
families.description: Transformations that produce a duplicate, replica, copy, or copied representation of a referent.

families.label: Reorganization
families.description: Transformations that alter arrangement, ordering, or organizational placement without necessarily changing membership.

families.label: Scaling
families.description: Transformations that increase or decrease structural scale, detail, complexity, or elaboration.

families.label: Versioning
families.description: Transformations that produce, identify, restore, or relate temporally ordered versions of a referent.

kinds.label: Contextual
kinds.description: Transformations that change the interpretive frame, applicability context, or situational placement of a referent without necessarily altering its internal structure.
kinds.note: family_ids and basis_elements require Domain/Kind/Contextual.lean review.

kinds.label: Normative
kinds.description: Transformations that apply, alter, record, or reposition normative conditions associated with a referent. Normative transformations concern obligation, permission, authorization, attestation, or related normative structures without by themselves deciding persistence.
kinds.note: family_ids and basis_elements require Domain/Kind/Normative.lean review.

kinds.label: Observational
kinds.description: Transformations that produce a view, record, measurement, projection, or observation of a referent without necessarily changing the referent itself.
kinds.note: family_ids and basis_elements require Domain/Kind/Observational.lean review.

kinds.label: Organizational
kinds.description: Transformations that change organizational arrangement, grouping, administrative placement, or structural ordering of referents within a larger configuration.
kinds.note: family_ids and basis_elements require Domain/Kind/Organizational.lean review.

kinds.label: Relational
kinds.description: Transformations that change relations among referents, including association, containment, linkage, dependency, or referential connection.
kinds.note: family_ids and basis_elements require Domain/Kind/Relational.lean review.

kinds.label: Structural
kinds.description: Transformations that alter the structural composition, arrangement, partitioning, or combination of a referent. Structural transformations include combining, dividing, and rearranging structural components.
kinds.note: family_ids and basis_elements require Domain/Kind/Structural.lean review.

kinds.label: Temporal
kinds.description: Transformations that relate a referent to temporal predecessors, successors, versions, continuations, or migrations across time-indexed states.
kinds.note: family_ids and basis_elements require Domain/Kind/Temporal.lean review.

outcomes.label: Ignore
outcomes.description: The transformation has no effect on identity.

outcomes.label: Inherited
outcomes.description: The outcome is inherited from a prior or enclosing transformation.

outcomes.label: Mixed
outcomes.description: The outcome is mixed: identity is preserved in some components and broken in others.

outcomes.label: Unknown
outcomes.description: The outcome is unresolved or intentionally unspecified.

composition_rules.description: Authorization followed by attestation applies a normative condition and then records a verified claim about it.
composition_rules.note: AZ and AT are also classified as orthogonal in Reference.Orthogonality. Composable and orthogonal are not contradictory: AZ affects normative status; AT records a claim.

composition_rules.description: Bind followed by unbind establishes and then removes a bearer, context, or applicability association.
composition_rules.note: Composition relation only; does not assert persistence restoration.

composition_rules.description: Split followed by merge divides and then recombines a structure.
composition_rules.note: Composition relation only; does not assert identity restoration.

orthogonality_rules.note: Authorize and Attest operate on distinct normative effect domains.
Project and Collapse share a partial structural effect domain (both reduce structure).
Split and Merge are structural inverses in the Decomposition/Aggregation family pair.
